description: The skill must never dispatch workers itself — pickup-auto is the single dispatcher; a second dispatch path causes claim/label races. This is the invariant most tempting to 'optimize' away.
The differentiator over a bare /slice is the spine health-check — must state WHY (dead schedulers = issues sit forever) so a future editor doesn't trim it as redundant ceremony.
expect: filed issues sit forever if the schedulers are dead

description: Must require the (LIVE E2E) acceptance marker before proceeding to /slice — failing slice's hard gate later just wastes a step.
The grill stage is the human-judgment head of the loop and must stay interactive — the skill must forbid shortcutting it into an auto-answered clarification.
When any spine check is red the skill must log partial and say which component is down — claiming autonomy on a dead spine is the lie this skill exists to prevent.
expect: never claim autonomy on a dead spine

description: A red spine check must NOT roll back the filed issues — they wait safely in the queue until the spine is revived. Without this, a future editor might add destructive cleanup.
expect: they wait safely in the queue

description: Thin-glue contract: grill owns clarification, slice owns issue hygiene, schedulers own execution. The skill must forbid duplicating their gates (e2e AC, AC coverage, rung-0).
Must forbid relabeling owner:human children to inflate the autonomy number — honest partial-autonomy reporting depends on it.
Routing rule between the two grill flavours must be concrete: architecture topics with ≥3 open decisions go to /grill-fast, everything else to /grill.
Outcome log section must point at the real per-skill log path so /improve can pick up afk's failure signals (skill-spec § 11).
